section_title: The Eleven Curtains of Goat Hair

versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 14 He then made curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven curtains in all. 15 Each of the eleven curtains was the same size—thirty cubits long and four cubits wide. 16 He joined five of the curtains into one set and the other six into another. 17 He made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set. 18 He also made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together as a unit.
19 Additionally, he made for the tent a covering of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of fine leather.

context_lines: In **Exodus 36**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Exodus.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Eleven Curtains of Goat Hair** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Exodus 36** remains the first control on meaning.
